_fixture_note: Real output from 2026-07-17 against a genuine, more complex throwaway test git repo (built and destroyed purely for this test, never the project repo): 2 real secrets, a decoy value, a real branch + merge (not a linear chain), and one secret deliberately also pasted into a commit message. This exact scenario is what surfaced the third real bug in this skill: git filter-repo --replace-text never touches commit messages, and the scanner never checked them either -- both fixed, and this output reflects the fixed, independently-verified-by-hand behavior. The plan_for_the_real_repo command now correctly includes --replace-message alongside --replace-text.

plan_for_the_real_repo.note: None of the steps below were run against your real repo or remote -- only against a disposable, deleted-afterward clone, to PROVE this plan actually works before you run it for real.
plan_for_the_real_repo.5_mandatory_warning: Every commit after the earliest rewritten one gets a new SHA. Every other clone/fork of this repo is now diverged -- everyone with a clone MUST delete it and re-clone fresh; a normal `git pull` will not work and can resurrect the purged secret.
